title: Struthiopteris chauliodonta
taxonomy: Struthiopteris chauliodonta is classified within the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, a group of ferns with a diverse range of species. At the order level, it is classified as Polypodiales, a large and widespread group of ferns. Within the family, it is placed in the Thelypteridaceae, a family of deciduous and evergreen ferns. As a species, Struthiopteris chauliodonta is further classified within the genus Struthiopteris, which is a group of ferns with distinctive characteristics.

intro: Struthiopteris chauliodonta, also known as the wavy-leaved water fern or struthiopteris fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Struthiopteridaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Struthiopteris chauliodonta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
